title: Backports and release notes for v5.8.0-RC1
body:
There's quite a bit here, but most of the machine changes are missing because of the refactoring done by Brent to make providers less prominent.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: The build failures don't repro locally. Go version issue, maybe?
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

@mheon Ok so this looks weird as hell but I can explain!

To reproduce you must run `make vendor` then you see that this is not "clean" and notice it pull in `github.com/containers/podman/v6`, the reason being in volume_test.go from https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/94f50324e0a2168ee89b683d3e7e80f6f952a3cb

That v6 bump then also causes upgrades to other dependencies such as securejoin which removed that symbol in the meantime as well as as the runtime-spec which switched the type to a pointer.

So in short make that v6 import v5 and then make sure make vendor is clean and then it should work.
The Build Each Commit test failed in 20 minutes due to a timeout.  There were a BOATLOAD of troff warnings, I don't know if all the warnings slowed things down too much?  Anyway, I have restarted.
One very minor nit, otherwise LGTM once the tests are happy.

> The Build Each Commit test failed in 20 minutes due to a timeout. There were a BOATLOAD of troff warnings, I don't know if all the warnings slowed things down too much? Anyway, I have restarted.

Rerunning wont help, the troff warnings are to be expected.

The main issue that this jobs as to compile 76 commits in a row to ensure every single one works and well that takes more than 20 mins. I guess the simple answer would be bump the timeout but then if the output is accurate and flushed it shows `Rebasing (56/152)` which means we would 3x the time to complete it most likely and 60mins for this job seems a bit much. I ma not sure if more cpu is going to help though

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index 7c61d8222c..02e14086a3 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -247,6 +247,10 @@ alt_build_task:
     matrix:
       - env:
             ALT_NAME: 'Build Each Commit'
+        gce_instance:
+            <<: *fastvm
+            cpu: 8
+        timeout_in: 60m
       - env:
             # TODO: Replace with task using `winmake` to build
             # binary and archive installation zip file.
```

totally untested but worth a shot
I just threw a 150 minute timer on, but your way would have been better...
Yeah, I'll do the Misc bit for final release
